contractSurface.securityClassification: platform tenant lifecycle orchestration, quota, policy, health, audit, isolation, and offboarding controls
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-30 keeps tenant-lifecycle-manager as the portability, UI, and package slug while the current runtime services live under server/src/engine/flows/tenant-lifecycle. Fork assembly copies that implementation directory into the canonical package slug path.
coreGuarantees: Tenant provisioning remains idempotent by tenant and returns queued status immediately.
Quota allocation, quota enforcement, config inheritance, and policy enforcement stay tenant scoped.
Isolation violations hard-stop with an auditable failure path.
Audit and offboarding records remain insert-only lifecycle evidence.
Offboarding is scheduled with a retention window and never immediate deletion.
Health scoring keeps bounded scores from 0 to 1.
Usage metrics aggregation remains asynchronous and off the live request path.
Lifecycle side effects preserve store-before-event ordering.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-30 can govern tenant provisioning, offboarding, quota, and isolation behavior; Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: tenant provisioning is idempotent by tenantId
provision records are stored before tenant.provisioned events
the live response remains queued and non-blocking
quota allocation events are emitted after persistence
quota tier names are tenant metadata, not isolation boundaries
resolution order remains global, plan, tenant override
effective config is resolved per tenant
resolution events are emitted after persistence
quota excess remains a hard stop
cross-tenant access attempts are always auditable
offboarding is scheduled rather than immediate
retention policy does not delete active tenant records
policy violation remains a guarded failure code

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-30 operating values. These values may tune lifecycle labeling, quota profile, template chain, audit and offboarding retention, health warning posture, and usage aggregation cadence without changing tenant isolation, hard-stop guards, async-only metrics, or store-before-event ordering.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to module naming, tenant lifecycle quotas, policy posture, retention windows, health thresholds, and usage aggregation windows.

edgeCases.name: Acme tenant ops
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes the module for enterprise tenant provisioning with higher quota limits and longer audit retention.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: quota tier, storage limit, audit retention, module naming
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Tenant isolation, guard failures, and store-before-event ordering remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind lifecycle control
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the installed package for tighter standard quota controls and shorter usage windows.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: quota limit, health threshold, usage aggregation window
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Quota excess remains a hard stop and metrics aggregation remains asynchronous.

edgeCases.name: Tessera tenant governance
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes the cascade package for governance-heavy offboarding and policy enforcement.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: retention window, policy profile, audit retention
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Offboarding remains scheduled and audit records remain insert-only.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T468 through T477 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1200 through F1219 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Tenant lifecycle reads and writes remain tenant scoped.
Tenant provisioning stays idempotent by tenantId.
Quota excess remains a hard stop.
Cross-tenant isolation violations remain hard stops and are auditable.
Offboarding remains scheduled and never immediate deletion.
Health scores remain bounded from 0 to 1.
Store-before-event ordering remains mandatory for lifecycle side effects.

tenantATenantOps.description: Tenant A tunes Tenant Lifecycle Manager for enterprise tenant operations.
tenantATenantOps.impact: Tenant A gets a higher-capacity lifecycle control package while tenant isolation, quota hard stops, scheduled offboarding, and audit immutability stay unchanged.
tenantATenantOps.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-30 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-30 package assembly copies the tenant-lifecycle implementation under the canonical tenant-lifecycle-manager package slug.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
